headline: Coding-agent tooling led the quarter as retrieval and debugging techniques broke out alongside a wave of agent integration moves.
body: The window's rotation centered on AI-assisted development, with code generation, task decomposition, prompt chaining, and session management all shifting together as context provision for coding agents drew direct attention [3]. Breakouts clustered around retrieval and code search infrastructure, with vector search, HNSW, and code indexing rising alongside new entrants in debugging, record and replay, and remediation actions. On the model side, Meituan released the 1.6 trillion parameter Mixture-of-Experts LongCat-2.0 trained on AI ASIC superpods rather than Nvidia GPUs [5], and Base44 began rolling out its own model as vibe coding platforms seek defensibility [9]. Agent integration remained contested, with OpenAI merging chat and agents into persistent agents [1] and Anthropic pursuing direct Microsoft Teams integration against Microsoft's Copilot [4][6].
